editionData.title: May 30, 2026: A court in Kenya blocks U.S. plan to keep American Ebola patients in Africa; After a brutal winter, Ukraine’s drones...
editionData.description: Global headlines: A court in Kenya blocks U.S. plan to keep American Ebola patients in Africa (A facility built by the U.S. military on a Kenyan air base was intended to isolate Americans exposed to Ebola during the growing outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo); After a brutal winter, Ukraine’s drones are breaking Russian defenses (Russia’s advance has suddenly stalled, and Ukraine is fighting on its own terms — a comeback credited to Kyiv’s efforts to steadily strengthen the capabilities of its UAVs); and A condemnation of Putin in glitzy Cannes still angers Ukrainians besieged by war (Andrei Zvyagintsev urged Russian President Vladimir Putin to end the “bloodbath” — but in Ukraine his remarks elicited ridicule and frustration). Markets leaned higher, with 4 of 7 tracked indexes up; Nikkei 225 up 2.53% was the largest move. Benchmarks tracked: S&P 500, NASDAQ Composite, Euronext 100 Index, and Public Joint-Stock Company Moscow Exchange MICEX-RTS plus 3 more indexes. Hacker News: SQLite is all you need for durable workflows (obeli.sk: SQLite is all you need for durable workflows); The dead economy theory (We can laugh at them but we have to take this seriously); and Naphtha Shortages Having a Growing Impact in Japan (The leading Japanese snack-food manufacturer Calbee has made headlines through its announced switch to black-and-white packaging for some...). Product Hunt features NODUS HN Radar (Track rising Hacker News posts before they explode), Sinalytica (Travel back to 1998 and use Lovable on Windows 98), and Drafted (Design a home instantly with AI).
editionData.author: Sony Mathew

headlines.title: A court in Kenya blocks U.S. plan to keep American Ebola patients in Africa
headlines.summary: A facility built by the U.S. military on a Kenyan air base was intended to isolate Americans exposed to Ebola during the growing outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo.

headlines.title: After a brutal winter, Ukraine’s drones are breaking Russian defenses
headlines.summary: Russia’s advance has suddenly stalled, and Ukraine is fighting on its own terms — a comeback credited to Kyiv’s efforts to steadily strengthen the capabilities of its UAVs.

headlines.title: A condemnation of Putin in glitzy Cannes still angers Ukrainians besieged by war
headlines.summary: Andrei Zvyagintsev urged Russian President Vladimir Putin to end the “bloodbath” — but in Ukraine his remarks elicited ridicule and frustration.

headlines.title: Russian drone hits building in NATO member Romania, drawing outcry from U.S., allies
headlines.summary: Romanian authorities said a Russian drone hit the roof of an apartment block and exploded, potentially raising tensions between Moscow and European nations.

headlines.title: Lebanon ceasefire falters as Trump pushes for Iran deal
headlines.summary: Israel is “deepening its operation” in Lebanon, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said.

headlines.title: Louisiana lawmakers pass congressional map favouring Republicans
headlines.summary: Louisiana approves new congressional map eliminating a majority-Black district after an April Supreme Court ruling.

headlines.title: Trump’s ‘final’ decision on Iran deal due; Israel deepens Lebanon invasion
headlines.summary: Israel pushes deeper into Lebanon just days after Israel's prime minister ordered 70 percent of Gaza to be occupied.

headlines.title: Trump pledges to withdraw from Kennedy Center after court strikes his name
headlines.summary: Judge Christopher Cooper ruled against Trump's bid to add his name to the building and close the theatre for two years.

headlines.title: ICE agent arrested over shooting of Venezuelan man in US immigration raid
headlines.summary: The charges stem from the January 14 shooting of Julio Cesar Sosa-Celis in Minneapolis during Operation Metro Surge.

headlines.title: ‘Arbitrary measures’: Lula slams US ‘terror’ designation for Brazil gangs
headlines.summary: Lula warned that labelling Brazil's criminal networks as 'terrorists' could 'undermine' local law enforcement efforts.

headlines.title: Trump's name must come off of the Kennedy Center, judge rules
headlines.summary: The judge wrote in his 94-page ruling that it was "crystal clear" that the arts complex was named for the late president John F. Kennedy. He also ruled that the center could not wind down its programming and close for two years of renovations – at least for now

headlines.title: Why are people attacking Ebola clinics? It revolves around trust, death and body bags
headlines.summary: In echoes of past outbreaks, community members are attacking clinics, distrusting doctors and following burial traditions that could lead to more cases of Ebola.

headlines.title: Louisiana lawmakers pass a congressional map to dismantle a majority-Black district
headlines.summary: Louisiana's Republican lawmakers raced to eliminate one of two majority-Black congressional seats in the state after the U.S. Supreme Court ruled the current map unconstitutional in a sweeping ruling.

headlines.title: Here's how we're coping with high gas prices, according to Costco and Walmart
headlines.summary: Sky-high gas prices have drivers going out of their way for discounts at the pump. Oil executives warn that even higher prices might be on the horizon.

headlines.title: Asia defense summit opens amid doubts over U.S. priorities
headlines.summary: The Shangri-La Dialogue, hosted by the International Institute for Strategic Studies, will also address tensions in the Middle East and Russia's war on Ukraine.

headlines.title: One German mayor's enduring love for Huangshan
headlines.summary: On what was China Daily reporter Xia Ji's first time in Huangshan, Anhui, she met a German mayor visiting the city for his fourth time. Alexander Badrow's connection to Huangshan dates back to 2015, when Huangshan and the Baltic Sea port of Stralsund became sister cities. Over the past decade, Badrow has collaborated with partners in Huangshan to preserve ancient architecture, safeguard cultural heritage and foster an enduring friendship. Click for more about why this German mayor remains deeply committed to Huangshan.

headlines.title: India swelters under severe hot weather
headlines.summary: An excruciating pre-monsoon heat wave is baking large parts of India, with temperatures routinely breaching 45 C and topping 47 C in many states, causing human and economic distress and even some deaths due to heatstroke.

headlines.title: Steel tariffs open 'can of worms', drive up prices
headlines.summary: The humble tin can is the latest victim of US steel tariffs as domestic manufacturers say producing cans at home has become more expensive than importing them, despite Washington's push to bolster steel production and limit imports.

headlines.title: France extends nuclear umbrella to Norway
headlines.summary: Norway will begin talks on joining a France-led nuclear deterrence program, the countries' leaders announced, a move that, if concluded, would be a notable shift for a traditionally Atlanticist nation long aligned with the United States on security.

headlines.title: China-US cooperation stressed after summit
headlines.summary: Leading scholars from China and the United States are stressing the importance of seizing the opportunity to improve bilateral ties ushered in by the summit between the two countries earlier this month.

headlines.title: Supreme Court sets 3-month deadline for High Courts to pronounce judgments after reserving orders
headlines.summary: The Supreme Court has imposed a strict three-month deadline for all the High Courts to deliver judgments in cases that have been reserved for decision.

headlines.title: AAP wins most wards in Punjab local polls, Opposition alleges misuse of govt. machinery
headlines.summary: Aam Aadmi Party (AAP) wins Punjab local polls amid allegations of government machinery misuse, with CM Mann touting a development-driven governance model.

headlines.title: Delhi High Court upholds TRAI regulations capping television ads to 12 minutes per hour
headlines.summary: Delhi HC upholds TRAI regulations capping television ads to 12 minutes per hour

headlines.title: Truck driver from West Bengal transporting rice to Ukhrul killed in Manipur ambush
headlines.summary: West Bengal truck driver killed in Manipur ambush; a police constable injured amid ongoing regional tensions and protests.

headlines.title: Naga body threatens to boycott Manipur government
headlines.summary: ANSAM demands the release of Naga hostages and action against Kuki extremists, threatening a boycott if unmet.

headlines.title: Exclusive: US general meets Cuban military officials at edge of Guantanamo Bay
headlines.summary: Reuters: Exclusive: US general meets Cuban military officials at edge of Guantanamo Bay.

headlines.title: Trump says he will 'transfer' Kennedy Center to Congress after court setback
headlines.summary: Reuters: Trump says he will 'transfer' Kennedy Center to Congress after court setback.

headlines.title: Zelenskiy says Russia is preparing major new attack on Ukraine
headlines.summary: Reuters: Zelenskiy says Russia is preparing major new attack on Ukraine.

headlines.title: Iran's strongest card in nuclear talks: its highly enriched uranium
headlines.summary: Reuters: Iran's strongest card in nuclear talks: its highly enriched uranium.

headlines.title: Ghana lawmakers approve bill criminalizing LGBTQ 'promotion', official says
headlines.summary: Reuters: Ghana lawmakers approve bill criminalizing LGBTQ 'promotion', official says.

hackerNews.title: Naphtha Shortages Having a Growing Impact in Japan
hackerNews.summary: The leading Japanese snack-food manufacturer Calbee has made headlines through its announced switch to black-and-white packaging for some of its products due to naphtha shortages, which are having a growing impact on a wide range of industries.

hackerNews.title: Notes from the Mistral AI Now Summit
hackerNews.summary: A few days in Paris for the Mistral AI Now Summit: open models, on-prem deployment, agentic harnesses, and why Mistral wants to be the European full-stack AI partner.

hackerNews.title: MCP is dead?
hackerNews.summary: TL;DR : MCP eats context, has low reliability, and overlaps with existing CLI/API.

hackerNews.title: Shift will clean homes for free to train future robots
It's hard to justify buying a Framework 12
hackerNews.summary: My nephew just graduated high school, and wants a laptop. When he decides what computer to buy, price (or more precisely, value) is the most important attribute. Apple

hackerNews.title: Show HN: Tiny-vLLM – high performance LLM inference engine in C++ and CUDA
hackerNews.summary: Build your own high performance LLM inference engine in C++ and CUDA - a smaller version of vLLM - jmaczan/tiny-vllm

hackerNews.title: WH proposes rules giving political appointees final approval on research grants
hackerNews.summary: These proposed Office of Management and Budget regulations would render the federal research grant review process opaque

hackerNews.title: Liquid AI reveals 8B-A1B MoE trained on 38T
hackerNews.summary: Today, we’re releasing LFM2.5-8B-A1B, a high-throughput edge model optimized for fast, reliable tool calling and complex instruction following on consumer hardware, delivering compressed performance competitive with much larger models and day-one support across major inference frameworks.
